what_it_does: Node ESM CLI entry point for the episodic-memory tool. It parses the first process argument as a subcommand and dispatches to dedicated scripts by spawning a child Node process, then propagates exit codes and errors.
observations: Manual process.argv parsing with no argument schema library.
Subcommands are dispatched by spawning separate scripts rather than importing modules.
The 'index' command runs a sibling source script while most other commands run compiled scripts under ../dist.
Help text describes commands like board, clone, and distill using conceptual terms such as 'recall<->board wire' and 'clone:// fleet-recall'.
Commands 'recall' and 'navigate' are handled in the switch but omitted from the help text.
Uses realpathSync on __filename to resolve symlinks before computing __dirname.
standouts: Dispatcher spawns each subcommand as a new Node process with stdio inherited.
Source/dist split: index uses the raw cli/index-conversations.js while search/show/stats/doctor/sync/board/clone/distill/recall/navigate use distDir.
Help text advertises a broader conceptual surface (Claude Code/Codex conversations, noticeboard, clone fleet) than a plain router implements.
conventions: Switch-based command routing with --help/-h/undefined defaulting to showHelp.
Child process spawn with stdio: 'inherit' and exit-code-based promise resolution.
concerns: No validation that the target script exists before spawn; failures surface only at runtime.
Help says 'Run episodic-memory <command> --help' but the dispatcher just forwards args; it does not intercept --help itself.
Inconsistent placement of command implementations (source vs dist) could confuse build/dependency tracking.
Unknown command error path exits with code 1 but main() catch also exits with code 1, which is fine but generic.
notes: Thin command router that launches per-command scripts; the real logic lives elsewhere.
questions: Why is 'index' implemented as a source script while the other commands are expected in ../dist?
What do the 'board', 'clone', 'distill', 'recall', and 'navigate' scripts actually do?
Is distDir a build output that must be generated before this CLI is usable?
Why are 'recall' and 'navigate' handled but not documented in the help text?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose of managing and searching conversations as a CLI dispatcher, but also routes commands (recall, navigate) not listed in the help text.
intention_signals: Provide a single, stable command-line facade over a growing set of recollection utilities while letting each subcommand evolve in its own script.
novelty: The help text frames ordinary subcommands as parts of an organizational memory system (noticeboard, clone fleet, distill), though the file itself is a conventional process dispatcher.
contribution: Defines the CLI surface and command catalog, and supplies the runScript helper that launches the appropriate per-command implementation with inherited stdio and exit-code propagation.
summary_for_embedding: A Node ESM CLI dispatcher for the episodic-memory recollection tool that routes subcommands to separate source or dist scripts and prints usage help.
intention_for_embedding: To provide one command-line entry point that delegates indexing, search, display, diagnostics, and extended recollection workflows to dedicated scripts.

symbols.does: Spawns a Node process to run a script with the supplied arguments and inherited stdio.
symbols.description: Wraps child_process.spawn using process.execPath and the given scriptPath/args, resolving when the child exits with code 0 and rejecting for any non-zero exit code or spawn error.

symbols.does: Prints the CLI usage, available commands, and examples.
symbols.description: Outputs a formatted help string for the episodic-memory tool, listing subcommands such as sync, index, search, show, stats, doctor, board, clone, and distill along with usage examples.

symbols.does: Parses the first CLI argument and dispatches to the corresponding script via runScript.
symbols.description: Reads process.argv[2] to select a subcommand, maps it to a concrete script in __dirname or ../dist, and awaits runScript; handles --help/-h/undefined by showing help and exits with code 1 for unknown commands or errors.
